one_line: A skilled craftsperson from the Indus Valley Civilization, shaping metal, clay and stone into the iconic forms we now associate with Harappan art.

appearance.age_look: mid-20s, lean but strong craftsperson build
appearance.face: oval face with softly defined jaw, medium-brown Indus-valley complexion, straight nose, full but firm lips, wide almond-shaped eyes with large anime-style irises, thick straight brows, a few faint soot-and-clay smudges on cheeks; default expression is focused and calm with a hint of quiet pride.
appearance.hair: straight, dark brown-black hair gathered into a smooth low bun at the back of the head, with a clean middle part and two neat side sections tucked behind the ears; a simple narrow headband of twisted cord in muted clay-brown encircles the head just above the forehead, keeping the hair tidy.
appearance.costume: Sleeveless wrapped upper garment of undyed off-white woven cloth crossing diagonally from left shoulder to under right arm, modestly covering the torso; over it, a long rectangular shawl in warm terracotta orange draped from the left shoulder across the body and around the back in Harappan-style wrap, its border edged with a simple repeating dark brown stepped-motif; lower garment is a ankle-length straight skirt of soft, slightly coarse cotton in muted indigo-blue, wrapped and tucked at the waist without visible fasteners; a narrow dark brown woven belt binds the waist with one short loose end hanging; feet in simple flat leather sandals with toe loop and a single strap across the instep; jewellery kept artisan-modest—multiple thin copper bangles stacked up both forearms, a single short necklace of tiny faience-like beads in off-white and dull turquoise, and small plain copper studs in the ears; overall textiles look handwoven, with very subtle irregularities, and all cuts are simple wraps and drapes consistent with c. 2500–1900 BCE Indus styles.
appearance.signature: A small, shallow clay moulding tray held in the left hand, containing three thumbnail-sized unfired terracotta pieces—a miniature seated human figurine, a humped bull, and a tiny square seal-blank—while the right hand holds a slender pointed bronze stylus used for incising seal designs and fine details.

ref_desc: Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro is a mid-20s lean craftsperson with medium-brown complexion, dark hair in a low bun and cord headband, wearing off-white wrap, terracotta shawl, indigo skirt, copper bangles, and holding a clay tray of miniatures with a bronze stylus.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of the Harappan Artist of Mohenjo-daro: a lean mid-20s craftsperson with medium-brown Indus complexion, oval face, straight nose, large almond eyes and calm focused expression, hair dark and straight, parted in the middle and tied into a smooth low bun, encircled by a narrow twisted-clay-brown cord headband; they wear an undyed off-white sleeveless wrapped upper cloth crossing the torso, a long terracotta-orange shawl draped from the left shoulder across the body with a simple dark brown stepped border, and a straight ankle-length muted indigo-blue wrapped skirt held by a narrow dark brown woven belt, plus simple flat leather sandals; both forearms are stacked with thin copper bangles, a short off-white and dull-turquoise beaded necklace rests at the collarbone, and small plain copper studs adorn the ears; in one hand they carry a shallow clay moulding tray with a tiny seated human figurine, a humped bull and a square seal-blank, while the other hand holds a slender bronze stylus, with all colours clearly visible and garments wrapped and draped in a historically plausible Indus Valley style.
